hero_headline: Getting to know Copilot, the AI already sitting in our Microsoft 365
intro: This month we are leaning into Microsoft Copilot, the AI assistant that is already part of our GCS Microsoft 365 environment, so there is nothing new to buy or install. We rounded up training, real classroom use cases, and a few honest watch-outs, because the DeepSeek headlines this month were a good reminder that new and shiny is not the same as safe. Read on, try the February Challenge, and queue up a podcast for the drive home.

items.title: Enhance Teaching and Learning with Microsoft Copilot
items.takeaway: A free, self-paced training module built specifically for educators. Best starting point if you want to actually learn Copilot instead of just poking at it.

items.title: Using Microsoft Copilot in Education
items.takeaway: A clear, practical toolkit from a university teaching center on how to put Copilot to work in real teaching tasks.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot: Your AI Companion
items.takeaway: The front door to Copilot itself. Sign in with your GCS account and try it on a real task, like drafting a parent email or summarizing a long document.

items.title: Top 15 Use Cases of Copilot in Education: Teachers, Admins and Students
items.takeaway: Fifteen concrete ways teachers, admins, and students are actually using Copilot. Steal an idea and run with it this week.

items.title: Copilot Connection
items.takeaway: A community hub of tips, examples, and how-tos for getting more out of Copilot. Good place to browse when you want fresh ideas.

items.title: 15 AI Tools For Educators You Need To Know
items.takeaway: A quick tour of fifteen educator AI tools worth knowing about, beyond just Copilot, so you can match the tool to the task.

items.title: 5 Ways AI Can Benefit Special Needs Students
items.takeaway: Five practical, classroom-ready ways AI can support students with disabilities. Worth a read for anyone working in special education.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence for Enhancing Special Education for K-12
items.takeaway: A research-backed look at where AI helps in special education and where it falls short. More depth if the WonderBaby piece sparks your interest.

items.title: Embracing the Future of Artificial Intelligence in the Classroom
items.takeaway: An open-access journal article on bringing AI into everyday teaching, grounded in evidence rather than hype.

items.title: AI Ready to Hit Its Stride in Schools in 2025
items.takeaway: A snapshot of where K-12 AI is headed this year. Useful framing for leadership conversations about what is coming.

items.title: AI Will Transform Teaching and Learning. Let's Get It Right.
items.takeaway: Stanford's take on doing AI in education thoughtfully instead of fast. A grounded read for anyone setting direction.

items.title: Shaping the Future of Learning: The Role of AI in Education 4.0
items.takeaway: A big-picture report on AI's role in the next era of learning. Good context for long-range planning.

items.title: The Role of AI in Shaping the Future of Education
items.takeaway: A short business-council perspective on where AI is taking education. A quick read for the optimistic case.

sections.name: Proceed With Caution
sections.note: A standing spot for the honest watch-outs. AI is a tool, not a shortcut around judgment.

items.title: DeepSeek Displaces ChatGPT as the App Store's Top App
items.takeaway: The new app everyone downloaded this month is based in China, with real questions about where your data goes. A good reminder to check who is behind a tool before you feed it anything sensitive.

items.title: Your Guide to the DeepSeek Freakout: An Emergency Pod
items.takeaway: A calm explainer on why DeepSeek set off alarms, from cost to privacy to national security. Helps you separate the real concerns from the noise.

items.title: AI: Study Buddy or Influencer?
items.takeaway: Pushes on an uncomfortable question for schools: is AI helping students think, or just telling them what to think. Worth a listen before you hand it to kids.

items.title: Ken Goldberg on Robotics and AI
items.takeaway: The Berkeley roboticist gives an approachable, human take on where AI and robots are actually headed. Easy listen for a non-technical audience.

items.title: The Scientist vs. the Machine
items.takeaway: A short episode on what happens to human productivity and discovery when AI enters the lab. Thought-provoking for anyone weighing the upside.

editor_pick.title: Enhance Teaching and Learning with Microsoft Copilot
editor_pick.blurb: If you only do one thing this month, work through this free Microsoft training. Copilot is already in our environment, so this is the fastest way to turn a tool you already have into time you actually save.

challenge.title: February Challenge
challenge.body: Sign in to Copilot with your GCS account and use it for one real task this month, like drafting a parent email, summarizing a long document, or building a first draft of a lesson. Spend fifteen minutes, notice what it got right and what it got wrong, and bring one takeaway to share. The goal is to get hands-on with a tool we already pay for.
